sections.about: This metric measures total waste generated per tonne of crude steel or physical output under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator on waste intensity. It is more comparable than absolute waste for steel companies of different scale. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: In FY2023, JSW led at 0.68 MT/tcs, followed by SAIL at 0.74 MT/tcs, Jindal at 0.7759 MT/tcs and Tata at 0.9 MT/tcs. In FY2025, SAIL led with 0.75 MT/tcs, Tata followed at 0.8 MT/tcs, JSW reported 0.85 MT/tcs and Jindal 0.95 MT/tcs. Tata improved consistently from 0.9 in FY2023 to 0.837 in FY2024 and 0.8 in FY2025, indicating steady production-normalised efficiency gains. Jindal's higher intensity despite lower absolute waste reflects a smaller denominator and stainless-route product-mix effects.
sections.tataPositioning: Tata ranked 4th in FY2023 at 0.9 MT/tcs, improved to 3rd in FY2024 at 0.837 MT/tcs, and further to 2nd in FY2025 at 0.8 MT/tcs. The consistent improvement across all three years indicates meaningful reduction in waste per tonne of steel produced.
sections.targets: Broader utilisation and landfill targets from SAIL and JSL are relevant but are not expressed as physical-output intensity targets.
sections.comparabilityNotes: FY2022-23 figures for all companies are restated values from FY2023-24 BRSR reports and were not reported in the original FY2022-23 BRSR disclosures.
